title: baccharis pycnocephala
intro: Baccharis pycnocephala, also known as Coyote Mint or Palo Amargo, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm various health issues. The medicinal properties of Baccharis pycnocephala have been found to be beneficial in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and bronchodilatory effects. Baccharis pycnocephala can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its health benefits, and is also used in topical applications for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
